verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass transmembrane protein (6 TM helices) that resides on intracellular membranes — Golgi apparatus, recycling endosomes, and phagophore assembly sites — where it acts as a phospholipid scramblase to nucleate autophagosome biogenesis. Its trafficking cycles between the TGN, endosomes, and pre-autophagosomal structures, but this is entirely intracellular vesicular cycling; no documented pool reaches the plasma membrane outer leaflet. There is no TM topology that exposes a domain to the extracellular space, no GPI anchor, no CD designation, no clinical surface-directed antibody/CAR-T program, and no published surface biotinylation or flow-cytometry data on intact cells. Pre-no checklist: no cancer/apoptosis outer-leaflet display reported; no germline-restricted surface expression; not a lysosomal exocytosis protein; no PM minority pool documented; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the outer leaflet. All contextual buckets lack gene-specific experimental evidence.
